overview: A URL shortener service with two backend APIs: the original shortener API that generates unique short codes and handles redirection, and a new analytics service that records redirect events. The shortener API communicates asynchronously with the analytics service via HTTP POST on each redirect to log source IP, user-agent, and timestamp. The analytics service exposes a GET endpoint to retrieve statistics per short code.
requirements: The API must accept a long URL via POST request and return a unique short code
The API must redirect users from short code to original long URL via GET request
Short codes must be unique across all stored URLs
The same long URL can be shortened multiple times, generating different short codes each time
Short codes must use alphanumeric characters (letters and numbers) only
Short codes must be compact in length
The system must handle collision detection to ensure uniqueness
The API must expose a /health endpoint for readiness checks
The redirect must use appropriate HTTP status code (301 or 302)
Every redirect event must be recorded with source IP, user-agent, and timestamp
The analytics service must expose GET /stats/{code} endpoint to retrieve redirect statistics
The analytics service must store redirect events persistently in-memory
The shortener API must send redirect events to the analytics service asynchronously

components.name: url-shortener-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: URL Shortener API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for creating short URLs and redirecting to original URLs
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /shorten:
    post:
      summary: Create a short URL
      operationId: shortenURL
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - url
              properties:
                url:
                  type: string
                  format: uri
                  description: The long URL to shorten
                  example: https://example.com/very/long/path/to/resource
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Short URL created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  shortCode:
                    type: string
                    description: The generated short code
                    example: abc123
                  shortUrl:
                    type: string
                    format: uri
                    description: The complete short URL
                    example: http://localhost:9090/abc123
                  originalUrl:
                    type: string
                    format: uri
                    description: The original long URL
                    example: https://example.com/very/long/path/to/resource
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: url field is required
        '500':
          description: Server error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: failed to generate short code
  /{shortCode}:
    get:
      summary: Redirect to original URL
      operationId: redirectToOriginal
      parameters:
        - name: shortCode
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: The short code to redirect
          example: abc123
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect to original URL
          headers:
            Location:
              schema:
                type: string
                format: uri
              description: The original long URL
        '404':
          description: Short code not found
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: short code not found
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a URL shortener service in Go using net/http on port 9090. Create three endpoints: GET /health returns {"status":"ok"} with 200 status. POST /shorten accepts JSON body with 'url' field containing the long URL to shorten. Generate a unique short code using alphanumeric characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9) with length 6-8 characters. Use a random generation approach and check for collisions against existing codes in storage. Store the mapping from short code to original URL in an in-memory map protected by a mutex for thread safety. Return 201 with JSON containing shortCode, shortUrl (full URL with short code), and originalUrl. Return 400 if url field is missing or invalid. Return 500 if code generation fails after reasonable retries. GET /{shortCode} looks up the short code in storage and returns 302 redirect with Location header set to the original URL. Return 404 with JSON error if short code doesn't exist. The same long URL shortened multiple times must generate different short codes each time (no deduplication). Use crypto/rand for generating random codes to ensure uniqueness. Implement proper error handling, request validation, and logging. Use standard library encoding/json for JSON handling. The service should be stateless except for the in-memory URL store. Include Dockerfile with multi-stage build using golang:1.21-alpine for build and alpine:latest for runtime. Expose port 9090. No external dependencies or databases required.

components.name: analytics-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Analytics Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Service for recording and retrieving URL redirect analytics
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /events:
    post:
      summary: Record a redirect event
      operationId: recordEvent
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - shortCode
                - sourceIp
                - userAgent
                - timestamp
              properties:
                shortCode:
                  type: string
                  description: The short code that was accessed
                  example: abc123
                sourceIp:
                  type: string
                  description: IP address of the requester
                  example: 192.168.1.1
                userAgent:
                  type: string
                  description: User-Agent header from the request
                  example: Mozilla/5.0
                timestamp:
                  type: string
                  format: date-time
                  description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the event
                  example: 2023-10-15T14:30:00Z
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Event recorded successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: recorded
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: missing required fields
        '500':
          description: Server error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: failed to record event
  /stats/{code}:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve statistics for a short code
      operationId: getStats
      parameters:
        - name: code
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: The short code to retrieve stats for
          example: abc123
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Statistics retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  shortCode:
                    type: string
                    example: abc123
                  totalClicks:
                    type: integer
                    example: 42
                  events:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: object
                      properties:
                        sourceIp:
                          type: string
                          example: 192.168.1.1
                        userAgent:
                          type: string
                          example: Mozilla/5.0
                        timestamp:
                          type: string
                          format: date-time
                          example: 2023-10-15T14:30:00Z
        '404':
          description: No statistics found for the short code
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: no stats found for code
        '500':
          description: Server error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    example: failed to retrieve stats
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement an analytics service in Go using net/http on port 9090. Create three endpoints: GET /health returns {"status":"ok"} with 200 status. POST /events accepts JSON body with required fields: shortCode (string), sourceIp (string), userAgent (string), and timestamp (ISO 8601 date-time string). Store each event in an in-memory data structure organized by shortCode. Use a map from shortCode to a slice of event objects, protected by a mutex for thread safety. Each event object contains sourceIp, userAgent, and timestamp. Return 201 with {"status":"recorded"} on success. Return 400 if any required field is missing or timestamp is invalid. Return 500 on internal errors. GET /stats/{code} retrieves all recorded events for the given short code. Return 200 with JSON object containing shortCode, totalClicks (count of events), and events array (all recorded events with sourceIp, userAgent, timestamp). Return 404 with JSON error if no events exist for the code. Return 500 on internal errors. Use standard library encoding/json for JSON handling. Parse and validate ISO 8601 timestamps using time.RFC3339. Implement proper error handling, request validation, and logging. The service should be stateless except for the in-memory event store. Include Dockerfile with multi-stage build using golang:1.21-alpine for build and alpine:latest for runtime. Expose port 9090. No external dependencies or databases required.
